metadata.name: Japanese Paragraphs
metadata.description: Learner-friendly multi-sentence Japanese reading passages with sentence-level chunking.

entries.title: 駅へ急ぐ朝
entries.ja: 山本さんは毎日渋谷で会社まで歩いて行きます。でも今日は遅くなったので、六時十五分の電車に乗ろうと思いました。階段を下りているとき、くつのひもがほどけているのに気づきましたが、電車も来るのが見えました。『走れ！』と思って、転ばないようにくつをぬいで、電車に乗ることができました。
entries.en: Mr. Yamamoto walks to his company in Shibuya every day. But today he ended up late, so he decided to try to catch the 6:15 train. While going down the stairs, he noticed his shoelace was untied, but he could also see the train arriving. He thought, 'Run!', took off his shoe so he would not trip, and managed to get on the train.
notes: Built as a short reading passage from familiar daily-life vocabulary and movement verbs.
The story is slightly playful, but the grammar stays learner-friendly.

sentences.ja: 山本さんは毎日渋谷で会社まで歩いて行きます。
sentences.en: Mr. Yamamoto walks to his company in Shibuya every day.

chunks.en: As for Mr. Yamamoto, every day in Shibuya

chunks.en: walks and goes to the company

entries.title: 雨の日の図書館
entries.ja: 午後に雨が強くなったので、私は図書館に入りました。窓の近くの静かな席で本を読んでいると、外の音が少し遠く感じられました。時間がゆっくり過ぎて、帰るころには気分も落ち着いていました。
entries.en: When the rain got stronger in the afternoon, I went into the library. As I read a book in a quiet seat near the window, the sounds outside felt a little far away. Time passed slowly, and by the time I went home, I felt calm again.
notes: This passage leans into reading-comprehension mood words rather than dialogue.

sentences.ja: 午後に雨が強くなったので、私は図書館に入りました。
sentences.en: Because the rain got stronger in the afternoon, I went into the library.

chunks.en: in a quiet seat near the window

chunks.en: my mood had calmed down too

entries.title: 土曜日の家族の買い物
entries.ja:
家族は土曜日の朝、近くのスーパーへ買い物に行きました。父は買い物リストを持ち、母は特売の野菜を探しています。高校生の娘は夕食にカレーを作りたいので、じゃがいもとにんじんをかごに入れました。小学生の弟はお菓子売り場に行きたがりましたが、今日は一つだけと約束しました。

店の中はとてもにぎやかで、魚売り場では新鮮なさけが並んでいました。母は夕飯用に魚も買うか少し迷いましたが、今日はカレーに決めました。父は牛乳やパンなど、毎日使う物を忘れないようにチェックしています。レジの前で、弟はチョコレートを選び、うれしそうに笑いました。

買い物が終わると、家族は重い袋を持って家に帰りました。家に着くと、娘はすぐに野菜を切り始め、母はサラダを作ります。父と弟はテーブルを準備しました。みんなで作った夕食はとてもおいしく、家族は楽しい一日だったねと話しながら食べました。
entries.en:
On Saturday morning, the family went shopping at a nearby supermarket. The father carried the shopping list, and the mother was looking for vegetables on special sale. Their high school daughter wanted to make curry for dinner, so she put potatoes and carrots into the basket. Their elementary school-age younger brother wanted to go to the snack aisle, but they agreed that he could choose only one thing today.

The store was very lively, and fresh salmon was lined up at the fish section. The mother hesitated a little about buying fish for dinner too, but decided on curry for today. The father was checking so he would not forget everyday items like milk and bread. In front of the register, the younger brother chose some chocolate and smiled happily.

When the shopping was finished, the family carried the heavy bags home. When they arrived, the daughter immediately started cutting vegetables, and the mother made a salad. The father and younger brother prepared the table. The dinner everyone helped make was very delicious, and the family ate while talking about what a fun day it had been.
notes: This passage uses common family and shopping vocabulary in a familiar weekend setting.
Several sentences highlight coordination and sequencing patterns useful for early reading practice.

entries.title: ゲームセンターの午後
entries.ja:
土曜日の午後、健太、美咲、そして亮は駅前のゲームセンターに行きました。

健太はゲームが大好きなので、入ってすぐにクレーンゲームの前に走りました。
「見て！あのぬいぐるみ、かわいい！」と美咲が言いました。
健太は何回も挑戦しましたが、なかなか取れませんでした。

その間、亮は音楽ゲームをしていました。
リズムに合わせてボタンを押しながら、楽しそうに笑っています。
「亮、すごいね。もうレベル十なの？」と美咲が聞きました。
「うん、毎週来てるからね」と亮は答えました。

しばらくして、三人はレースゲームで対戦することにしました。
健太は速く走りすぎて、何度も壁にぶつかりました。
美咲は慎重に運転して、一番になりました。

ゲームが終わったあと、三人は休憩スペースでジュースを飲みました。
「今日は楽しかったね。また来よう！」と健太が言いました。
「次は絶対クレーンゲームで勝つ！」と笑いながら言うと、みんなも笑いました。

夕方になり、三人はまた来る約束をして、駅へ向かいました。
entries.en:
On Saturday afternoon, Kenta, Misaki, and Ryo went to the arcade in front of the station.

Because Kenta loves games, he ran straight to the crane game as soon as they went in. "Look! That stuffed toy is cute!" Misaki said. Kenta tried many times, but he could not win it easily.

Meanwhile, Ryo was playing a music game. He pressed the buttons in time with the rhythm and smiled as if he was having a great time. "Ryo, you're amazing. Are you already on level ten?" Misaki asked. "Yeah, I come here every week," Ryo answered.

After a while, the three decided to compete in a racing game. Kenta drove too fast and crashed into the walls many times. Misaki drove carefully and came in first.

After the games were over, the three drank juice in the rest area. "Today was fun. Let's come again!" Kenta said. "Next time I'll definitely win at the crane game!" he said with a laugh, and everyone laughed too.

As evening came, the three promised to return and headed toward the station.
notes: This passage uses a familiar arcade setting with light dialogue and action verbs.
It mixes sequencing, quoted speech, and adverbial phrasing in a learner-friendly way.
